name: ruby-async
title: ruby-async
description:
concurrency framework for Ruby.
Async is a modern concurrency framework for Ruby. It implements the
reactor pattern, providing both non-blocking I/O and timer events.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ruby-async because concurrency framework for Ruby.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ruby-async as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ruby-async with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ruby-async --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
